s_ch: 身着银鬃铁卫制服的女性
t_ch: 小朋友们，不要到处乱跑啦。到这边集合……
t_en: Alright children, no running around, okay? Let's gather over here...

s_ch: 身着银鬃铁卫制服的女性
t_ch: 自我介绍一下，我是银鬃铁卫的情报官，也是「贝洛伯格历史之旅」的临时向导，佩拉格娅•谢尔盖耶夫娜。
t_en: So, let me introduce myself. I'm the Intelligence Officer for the Silvermane Guards, and a temporary guide for the "A Journey Through Belobog's History" program. My name is Pelageya Sergeyevna.

t_ch: 大家叫我佩拉就好。
t_en: But you can call me Pela.

t_ch: 佩～拉～姐～姐～
t_en: Yes~ Miss~ Pela~

t_ch: 佩～拉～姐～姐～
t_en: Yes~ Miss~ Pela~

t_ch: …请个别大人不要若无其事地模仿小孩子！
t_en: ...And perhaps the grownups among us could refrain from acting like children?

t_ch: 佩拉格娅•谢尔盖…
t_en: Pelageya Sergeyevna...

t_ch: …请个别大人旁听就好，这是针对孩子的历史之旅……
t_en: ...And perhaps the grownups among us could remain silent? This is a children's program...

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 好，小朋友们请看：这就是「永冬铭碑」，它是为了纪念伟大的筑城者而竖立的。
t_en: Okay guys, take a look over here: This is Everwinter Monument. It was erected to commemorate the mighty Architects.

t_ch: 筑城者是星神克里珀的追随者。是他们预见了危机，并筑起高墙，建立贝洛伯格，存护了人类文明的火种。直到如今，他们依然在管理着这座城市。
t_en: The Architects are followers of the Aeon Qlipoth. It was they that foresaw crisis, built up our walls, and established Belobog — preserving the spark of human civilization. Even today, they are still tending to the city.

t_ch: 永冬铭碑的碑体由两部分构成：象征知识与工业力量的齿轮，以及象征寒潮的巨大冰晶。这两个符号紧密嵌合，齿轮束缚住了冰霜，代表着筑城者们不屈服于蛮荒自然的精神。
t_en: Everwinter Monument is made up of two parts: A gear that symbolizes knowledge and industrial strength, and huge ice crystals that symbolize the Eternal Freeze. These two symbols are bound together — the gear restricting the ice. It represents the unyielding spirit of the Architects in the face of nature's barbarity.

t_ch: 大家还有什么问题吗～
t_en: Any questions~?

t_ch: 大家还有什么问题吗～
t_en: Any questions~?

t_ch: 纪念碑有什么象征含义吗？
t_en: Does the monument have any symbolic meaning?

t_ch: 纪念碑的碑体由两部分构成：象征知识与工业力量的齿轮，以及象征寒潮的巨大冰晶。这两个符号紧密嵌合，齿轮束缚住了冰霜，代表着筑城者们不屈服于蛮荒自然的精神。
t_en: The monument is made up of two parts: A gear that symbolizes knowledge and industrial strength, and huge ice crystals that symbolize the Eternal Freeze. These two symbols are bound together — the gear restricting the ice. It represents the unyielding spirit of the Architects in the face of nature's barbarity.

t_ch: 蓝色的结晶体是什么？
t_en: What are those blue crystals?

t_ch: 你是问材料吗？那是改变了颜色的地髓。地髓的颜色跟加工时的环境温度有关。
t_en: You mean, what are they made of? It's Geomarrow that's undergone a change in color. The color of Geomarrow is related to the environmental temperature during its refinement.

t_ch: 筑城者是什么人？
t_en: Who are the Architects?

t_ch: 筑城者们是星神克里珀的追随者。是他们预见了危机，并筑起高墙，建立贝洛伯格，存护了人类文明的火种。
t_en: The Architects are followers of the Aeon Qlipoth. It was they that foresaw the crisis, built up our walls, and established Belobog — preserving the spark of human civilization.

t_ch: 换句话来说，筑城者们既是贝洛伯格的奠基人，也是贝洛伯格的救世主。现如今，他们依然在管理着这座城市。
t_en: Or to put it another way, the Architects are Belobog's founders and saviors. Even today, they are still tending to the city.

t_ch: 寒潮是怎么出现的？
t_en: How did the Eternal Freeze occur?

t_ch: 这个问题应该去问科研部的专家，虽然他们可能也没法给出准确的答案。
t_en: That's a question for the Scientific Research Division, though they may not be able to give you a definitive answer.

t_ch: 寒潮似乎并非简单的自然灾害，百年前的气象学记录也无法解释它的成因。简单来说，这是困扰了贝洛伯格科学家们好几个世纪的难题。
t_en: The Eternal Freeze is no ordinary natural disaster — meteorological records from hundreds of years ago cannot explain the cause. To put it simply, it's a problem that has plagued Belobog scientists for centuries.

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 小朋友们还有什么想问的吗？慢慢来，时间还有很多。
t_en: And what about you guys? Take your time, we're not in a rush.

t_ch: 啊，终于回来啦！我都忘了新鲜空气是啥味道了……
t_en: Ah, we're finally back! ...I forgot what fresh air tasted like... Nom, nom, nom.

t_ch: 我们现在去哪儿？
t_en: Where do we go now?

t_ch: 你再等等，让我再体验一下……体验完了，走吧。
t_en: Wait! I haven't finished yet... Alright, let's go.

t_ch: 是啥味道？
t_en: What flavor is it?

t_ch: 自由的味道…寒冷的味道…有点冷了，还是走吧。
t_en: ...Freedom... Cold... Mainly cold actually. Let's go.

t_ch: 有没有觉得少了什么？
t_en: Aren't we missing something?

t_ch: 没有。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 桑博不见了——
t_en: Where's Sampo...

t_ch: 没有，下个话题。
t_en: Nope. Next question.

t_ch: 咱们接着去哪？先找个落脚的地方比较好，但我可不敢再去歌德宾馆了……
t_en: Where's our next stop? We should find somewhere to put our feet up, as long as it's not Goethe Hotel again...

t_ch: 桑博那家伙，大概是猜到我们会问他要藏身处，一回到行政区就溜没影了。真不能指望他。
t_en: Classic unreliable Sampo — he probably guessed we'd ask where his hiding place was. No sooner are we back in the Administrative District than he sneaks off.

t_ch: 最危险的地方也最安全，宾馆！
t_en: We should hide in plain sight. To the hotel!

t_ch: 不行啦！歌德老板认识我们，铁卫的驻扎点就在旁边一百米不到，你这是送货上门啊！
t_en: No way! The boss will recognize us, plus Silvermane Guards are stationed nearby. We'd be offering ourselves up on a silver platter!

t_ch: 在家靠父母出门靠朋友，朗道！
t_en: A friend in need is a friend indeed. Let's find Landau!

t_ch: 哪个朗道，姐姐还是弟弟？杰帕德的话，还是谨慎点为好…他可是铁卫高层啊，我们在他看来，没准就是会走路的军功章……
t_en: The bro or the sis? If you're talking about Gepard, we should be careful... He's a top Silvermane Guard — he'd probably get a medal for every one of us he brought in.

t_ch: 青山不改绿水长流，回列车！
t_en: Home is where the heart is. Back to the Express!

t_ch: 你这家伙真不讲义气！但我居然有点心动……
t_en: Where's your sense of loyalty!? Uh, that would be nice though...

t_ch: 丹恒，你怎么看？
t_en: Dan Heng, what do you think?

t_ch: 依我之见，先拜访希露瓦•朗道比较好。
t_en: I think it'd be best to pay a visit to Serval Landau.

t_ch: 果然，你也觉得不该现在去见杰帕德吧？
t_en: So you think we should avoid Gepard for the time being, right?

t_ch: 他是银鬃铁卫的高阶军官，想接触他恐怕没那么容易。而且…万一他没有被布洛妮娅的手书说动，我们等于是羊入虎口。
t_en: He's an elite officer of the Silvermane Guards. It wouldn't be easy to get to him. Besides... if he hasn't been persuaded by Bronya's letter, we'd be walking right into the lion's den.

t_ch: 那听你的？先去机械屋找希露瓦吧。
t_en: Hmm, makes sense... Guess we better head over to Serval's workshop then?

t_ch: 嗯。行动低调点，我们现在仍是被通缉的状态。
t_en: Mm. But... we must keep a low profile — we're still wanted fugitives.

t_ch: 怎么啦，希儿？
t_en: What's wrong, Seele?

t_ch: 没什么，只是有点…陌生。
t_en: Nothing, it's just a little... new.

t_ch: 上次来到地上时，我还是个小孩子。现在眼前的这些，我完全没有印象。
t_en: The last time I was on the surface, I was still a child. I have no memory of any of this.

t_ch: 上下层被隔绝实在太久了…太久了。
t_en: The Overworld and the Underworld... We've... We've been cut off for so long.

t_ch: 我没事，走吧。
t_en: I'm okay. Let's go.

t_ch: 巡逻的铁卫好多，平时也这样吗？
t_en: There are so many Silvermane Guards on patrol. Is it usually like this?

t_ch: 不，记得此前城里的戒备还没这么森严…如果这不是常态，也许与我们有关。
t_en: No, I don't remember the security being this tight last time... It might have something to do with us.

t_ch: 大守护者应该已经知道了我们的到来，布洛妮娅……
t_en: The supreme guardian has probably learned of our arrival already. Bronya...

t_ch: 喂！谁在那儿？
t_en: Hey! Who goes there?

t_ch: 糟糕，被发现了！
t_en: Darn, he's spotted us!

t_ch: …没办法，只好让他闭嘴了。
t_en: ...Well, now we have to help him keep his mouth shut.

t_ch: 给我站住，例行检查！
t_en: Halt, routine inspection!

t_ch: 嘁，别挡我们的路！
t_en: Tsk, don't stand in our way!

t_ch: 入、入侵者！得、得去通知警卫官——
t_en: Intruders! I must inform the security captain...

t_ch: 他想通风报信，阻止他！
t_en: He's gonna raise the alarm, stop him!

t_ch: 谁呀？今天不营业，明天再来吧！
t_en: Who is it? Shop's closed today, you'll have to come back tomorrow!

t_ch: 是我们呀，帮你修过东西的。
t_en: It's the radiator fixers.
Hah...?

t_ch: 来大客户咯，可以长期合作！
t_en: It's a big customer with bigger pockets!

t_ch: 没兴趣，我缺时间不缺钱！
t_en: Ha, not interested — I need time, not money!

t_ch: 希露瓦！我们是之前路过你这儿的，还帮你修过东西——记得嘛？
t_en: Serval! We came past here before and helped you fix a few things, remember?

t_ch: 什么——
t_en: What...?

t_ch: 啊，我想起来了！从贝洛伯格外面来的那三个！哎呀，你们怎么还在城里乱跑，不知道铁卫正满大街找你们吗？
t_en: Ah, I remember! The three outsiders! Wait — why are you still strolling around the city? Do you know the Silvermane Guards are looking all over for you?

t_ch: 快，快点进来。动作自然点，别被人盯上！
t_en: Quick, act natural and get inside. Don't get spotted!

t_ch: 行了，到我这儿你们就安全了。我这机械屋隔音还行，平时也没人会来。
t_en: Alright, you'll be safe here. My workshop has pretty good soundproofing, and barely anyone comes in.

t_ch: 你们到底犯了什么事儿啊，铁卫破天荒从前线调回一批，满城巡逻搜捕你们，整个上层区都人心惶惶的。
t_en: Just what crime did you commit exactly? They recalled a bunch of Guards from the front line to track you down — it's unprecedented. Now they're patrolling the whole city and everyone's in a panic.

t_ch: 我们……
t_en: Oh, we just—

t_ch: 先等一下，三月。
t_en: Not yet, March.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅说，我们可以完全信任你。
t_en: Bronya said we could trust you completely.

t_ch: 你这话里有话啊。强调布洛妮娅信任我，意思就是你还没信任我啰？
t_en: You mean to say that you don't trust me completely?

t_ch: 不错啊，够机警的！放心吧，我跟可可利亚说不上话，当年把我赶出筑城者的就是她，我犯不着站在她那边。
t_en: Good! That's smart of you. But you needn't worry, Cocolia and I aren't even on speaking terms. It was her that ejected me from the Architects — I refuse to stand with her.

t_ch: 除非筑城者公开确凿的证据，证明你们罪有应得——否则我不觉得你们会干坏事。而且以我对筑城者的了解，要有证据他们早就公布了。
t_en: I don't believe you guys would do anything bad, and the Architects are gonna need conclusive and publicly available evidence for me to think otherwise. Besides, I know the Architects, and if they did have any evidence, they would've released it by now.

t_ch: 这套我熟悉…可可利亚嘛，她办事就是这个风格，先让大家都忙活起来再说。
t_en: I know Cocolia's style — get everyone into a frenzy first.

t_ch: ——毕竟忙起来后，还有心思琢磨细节的人就不多了。
t_en: ...Most people in a frenzy don't stop to ponder the details.

t_ch: 你和可可利亚很熟？
t_en: Do you know Cocolia well?

t_ch: 岂止是熟，呵呵…我们曾经是形影不离的好姐妹。当然了，那是在她当上大守护者之前的事了……
t_en: Hehe, you could say that... We were like sisters once — inseparable. Of course, that was all before she became the supreme guardian...

t_ch: 那你琢磨出什么了吗？
t_en: So what details have you been pondering?

t_ch: 我？当然没有。可可利亚跟筑城者想做什么不关我事，我的时间这么宝贵，琢磨那个干嘛。
t_en: Me? None. What Cocolia and the Architects get up to has nothing to do with me. My time is too precious for pondering.

t_ch: 你很讨厌可可利亚吗？
t_en: Do you dislike Cocolia?

t_ch: 讨厌？我们曾经可是形影不离的好姐妹。当然了，那是在她当上大守护者之前的事了……
t_en: Dislike? We were like sisters once — inseparable. Of course, that was all before she became the supreme guardian...

t_ch: 现在的我嘛，就窝在这机械屋里修修破烂，玩玩音乐，过得挺自在的。
t_en: These days I just spend my time fixing things in this workshop and playing music. It's a pretty carefree existence.

t_ch: 哎呀，我可没打算跟你们聊不堪回首的过去，还是说回你们吧。消失好久的布洛妮娅让你们来找我，一定有很重要的事吧？
t_en: *sigh* If I'm not careful I'll start boring you all with sob stories from the past. Let's get back to you guys. If long-lost Bronya told you to come here, you must have something important to tell me?

t_ch: 你们可以信任我，虽然只有一面之缘，但直觉告诉我你们不是恶人。哈，我的直觉可是很准的喔。
t_en: You can trust me. I know our paths have only crossed once before, but my intuition tells me you guys are good people. And I've got good intuition by the way, haha.

t_ch: 一五一十，事无巨细地讲吧。
t_en: We may as well tell the long and short of it from start to finish.

t_ch: 讲故事我最擅长，我来！
t_en: Ooh! I'm the best storyteller — let me! Let me!

t_ch: …别添油加醋太多。
t_en: ...Try not to embellish the facts.

t_ch: 长话短说，言简意赅地讲吧。
t_en: We may as well cut to the chase and make a long story short.

t_ch: 讲故事我最擅长，我来！
t_en: Ooh! I'm the best storyteller — let me! Let me!

t_ch: …还是我来吧，你怕不是要讲到明天。
t_en: ...Perhaps I should do it, or we'll still be here tomorrow.

t_ch: 将在下层区的见闻和有关「星核」的信息告诉了希露瓦，并转交了布洛妮娅的信件……
t_en: Telling Serval about your time in the Underworld and what you know about the Stellaron, and showing her Bronya's letter...

t_ch: 怪不得最近都没有布洛妮娅的消息，原来还有这么段故事。下层区啊，封闭好多年了，很久没人上来过，没想到是这样……
t_en: So that's what's been happening — no wonder we hadn't heard from Bronya recently. *sigh* After all these years in isolation and without a single word from below, this is what's become of the Underworld...

t_ch: 我相信你们的经历，这么曲折离奇，自己可编不出来，何况还有这位希儿小姐现身作证。「星核」…呵，我知道布洛妮娅为什么要你们找我了。
t_en: I believe you, there's no way you could have invented all those twists and turns. What's more, we have Miss Seele here as living proof. As for the Stellaron... heh, I know why Bronya got you to find me.

t_ch: …我还是筑城者的一员、在科研部工作的时候，研究的就是「星核」。
t_en: ...When I was still one of the Architects working in the Scientific Research Division, I was researching the Stellaron.

t_ch: 不曾想被逐出了筑城者，还会再听到这个词…来吧，我把知道的都告诉你们。
t_en: I never thought I'd hear that word again after being expelled by the Architects... Come on, I'll tell you everything.

t_ch: 「星核」的存在，贝洛伯格没几个人知道；就算知道有这么个东西，也不会把它跟裂界和寒潮的源头扯到一起。
t_en: Only a very few people in Belobog know of the existence of the Stellaron. Those that do would never associate it with the Fragmentum or Eternal Freeze.

t_ch: 但根据那个机器人「史瓦罗」存储的数据…筑城者其实早就挖掘出了真相，只是研究结果被刻意藏了起来，确保不被外界知晓。
t_en: But according to the data recorded in that robot, Svarog... the Architects dug out the truth long ago. The research results were purposely hidden away to ensure that the outside world would never know of them.

t_ch: 是我运气不好呀…那么多选题，我偏偏选了「星核」。看来，任何想要触碰「星核」真相的人，都会被驱逐和处理掉吧。
t_en: Just my luck... Out of all the research topics I could've chosen, I insisted on the Stellaron. It's clear now that anyone who wanted to get closer to the truth would have been expelled or "abandoned."

t_ch: 或许我还要感谢可可利亚，她只是赶走了我，没有采取更一劳永逸的手段……
t_en: Maybe I should be thanking Cocolia. All she did was push me out... She could've decided to take a more permanent measure.

t_ch: 你对「星核」了解多少？
t_en: How much do you know about the Stellaron?

t_ch: 我算出了「星核」与「裂界」有关，离真相已经很近了。可惜还没有证明「星核」是裂界的起源，就被逐出了筑城者。
t_en: I figured out that the Stellaron and the Fragmentum were linked, so I was already close to the truth. It's a shame that the Architects ejected me before I could discover that the source of the Fragmentum was the Stellaron itself.

t_ch: 被革职之前，我正在申请现场勘测「星核」。虽然它是我的课题，但我从没亲眼见过它。所有研究都基于历史资料，和在实验室里搭建的模拟场景。
t_en: Before I lost my position, I was applying for permission to survey the Stellaron directly. It was my research topic, but I'd never even set eyes on it. My research was all based on historical data and lab simulations.

t_ch: 初步的研究报告和申请提交后不多久，我的处分就下来了。很明显，有人不希望研究继续。
t_en: Not long after I submitted my preliminary report and application, I received my punishment. It was obvious that somebody didn't want my research to continue.

t_ch: 知道可可利亚的目的吗？
t_en: Do you know what Cocolia's end goal is?

t_ch: 可可利亚…我们已经好多年没说过话了。曾经我和她是很好的朋友，彼此之间无话不谈。
t_en: Cocolia... It's been so many years since we last spoke. We were great friends once — there was nothing we wouldn't share.

t_ch: 但从坐上大守护者的位置开始，她就完全变了一个人。她以前秉持的信念，还有身上那股热情…就好像被某种力量抽出了躯壳。
t_en: She became a completely different person when she took on the supreme guardianship. The convictions she once held, her enthusiasm... it was like they were drawn out of her by some mysterious force.

t_ch: 她亲自下达驱逐我的命令，一面都不肯见我…她后来的行为，我不是很了解，早就放弃用正常的逻辑揣摩她了。
t_en: She gave the order to expel me herself and refused to see me... I never understood that behavior, and I gave up trying to use logic to work it out a long time ago.

t_ch: 有些时候，我甚至怀疑她其实是另一个人…有没有这种可能呢？说不定守护者是代代相传的意识，真正的可可利亚已经消失了。
t_en: Sometimes I even wonder whether she really did become a different person... is that possible? Perhaps there's a single guardian's consciousness that gets handed down from generation to generation... and the real Cocolia has already vanished.

t_ch: 是一代又一代的大守护者驱使她做出那些难以理解的决策？不知道，或许可以用这个点子写一首歌。
t_en: Is she being compelled by previous generations of guardians to make such baffling decisions? Who knows... but this might make for good songwriting material.

t_ch: 我们能和杰帕德谈谈吗？
t_en: Can we talk to Gepard?

t_ch: 杰帕德？可别…他人很正直，但可是出了名的一根筋。
t_en: Gepard? Not a chance... He's a decent guy, but also famously uncompromising.

t_ch: 我老弟把命令看得比什么都重要，就算你把布洛妮娅的信怼到脸上，他也不会动摇的。
t_en: For my brother, orders are more important than anything. Even if you stuck Bronya's letter to his forehead, he wouldn't be swayed.

t_ch: 跟他说话得靠技巧，要说服他，恐怕还得我出马才行——不过我现在还没想好策略。
t_en: You need to choose your words carefully if you want to persuade him of something. It might be best if I talk to him... though I'd need a strategy first.

t_ch: 你知道「星核」在哪里吗？
t_en: Do you know where the Stellaron is?

t_ch: 知道啊。虽然没亲眼见过，但我利用模拟实验和外派勘测做了粗略的定位。
t_en: Yes. I never observed it directly, but I used lab simulations and outbound surveys to establish a rough location.

t_ch: 根据勘测结果，「星核」大概位于贝洛伯格的北方——也就是茫茫雪原的某处。
t_en: According to the survey results, the Stellaron is likely to the north of Belobog... somewhere in the vast Snow Plains.

t_ch: 我们必须找到星核，而且越快越好——最好在可可利亚有所行动之前。你能给我们指个方向吗？
t_en: We must locate it as soon as possible — ideally before Cocolia takes action. Can you tell us the way?

t_ch: 没问题，我正这么打算呢！但是，这条路可不是嘴上说说那么简单啊。
t_en: Not a problem, I was thinking the same thing! But I'm afraid telling you might not be enough.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格以北几乎被裂界完全吞没了。要前往北部雪原，首先得穿过银鬃铁卫的前线禁区。
t_en: The area to the north of Belobog has been more or less swallowed up by the Fragmentum. If you want to get to the northern Snow Plains, you'll need to get past the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone on the front lines first.

t_ch: 就算平安穿过铁卫禁区——还有一大片裂界挡在前边。
t_en: Even if you get past the restricted zone in one piece... you'll have a whole heap of Fragmentum to deal with.

t_ch: 铁卫禁区？
t_en: Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone?

t_ch: 对。那是银鬃铁卫主力部队的驻扎地，他们在那里整顿部队，抵抗裂界的入侵。
t_en: Yes. That's where the main force of the Silvermane Guards is stationed. They've consolidated their position there in order to resist the encroaching Fragmentum.

t_ch: 大片裂界…
t_en: A whole heap of Fragmentum...

t_ch: 对。而且跟你们描述的地下小镇可不一样，是真正支离破碎、毫无生息的裂界。
t_en: Correct. And it's different from the underground towns you described. The Fragmentum there has quite literally torn everything to pieces — there's no sign of life, despite the monsters.

t_ch: 听上去…就我们仨加上希儿，不大够啊。要不要回去地下，让「地火」这就跟我们会合？
t_en: It sounds like... The three of us and Seele might not be enough. Why don't we go back underground and get Wildfire to come with us?

t_ch: 时间怕是不允许。
t_en: I fear we may not have the time.

t_ch: 嗐，也别这么悲观！我干公职的时候常在北边转悠，跟前线的士兵军官混得可熟了。
t_en: Hey, enough pessimism! Back in my "official capacity" days, I spent a lot of time in the north — I got to know the soldiers and officers on the front line very well.

t_ch: 硬攻不成，不妨智取。这样吧，我亲自带你们过去，如何？
t_en: This calls for brainwaves, not "brawnwaves." How about I take you over myself?

t_ch: 真的吗？好耶！有熟人带路，一下心里就有底了！
t_en: Really? Great! Phew, it's less scary if someone you know is leading the way!

t_ch: 哈哈，毕竟我也想亲眼看看「星核」啊——那可是我研究了十多年的课题，人这一辈子才有几个十年呀？
t_en: Haha, I wanna see the Stellaron too, y'know — it was my research topic for over a decade. You don't get that many decades in life.

t_ch: 那就这么定了！事不宜迟，准备好了咱们就出发。
t_en: That's settled then! There's no time to lose — we should head out as soon as you're ready.

t_ch: 糟、糟了！我忘了老弟说今天会来……
t_en: D—Darn it! I forgot my brother said he'd be around today...

t_ch: 你们快找地方躲起来——我来应付他！
t_en: Find somewhere to hide — quick — I'll handle him!

t_ch: 杰、杰帕德——你来了啊！我还以为前线这么紧张，你没空过来呢，哈哈……
t_en: G—Gepard, it's you! I didn't think you'd have the time to visit with things so tense on the front line, hehe...

t_ch: 还好，这阵子怪物的攻势有所缓和，我先回城里处理点事务，晚些再返回前线…之前不是告诉你了吗？
t_en: Things are manageable, the latest wave of monster attacks has slowed. I'm back in the city to take care of a few matters, but I'll be back on the front line later... I thought I told you?

t_ch: 啊？噢，对，是有这么回事……
t_en: Ah? Oh, yes, that's right...

t_ch: …姐，你气色不太对，发生什么了吗？
t_en: ...Sis, you look a bit pale. Did something happen?

t_ch: 没、没有的事，你从哪儿看出来的？
t_en: N—No, why would anything have happened?

t_ch: 你通常不叫我的大名。
t_en: You don't normally use my full name.

t_ch: 哎呀，你不是一直很反感我当众叫你「老弟」嘛！我觉得你说得对，平时还是用名字称呼你比较好，对吧老弟。
t_en: *sigh* You said you hate it when I call you "bro" in public! Well I agree. It'd sound better if I started using your full name. Right, broseph?

t_ch: …想怎么叫随便你吧。姐姐，今天我是为公事来的。「壁垒」装置出了故障，部队里的工程师无从下手。还是得找你看看。
t_en: ...You can call me whatever you like. Look Serval, I'm here on official business today. There's something wrong with the Barrier Generator Device and the engineers in our unit don't know how to fix it. I need you to take a look.

t_ch: 那帮人思维都僵硬了，当然看不懂我的设计。嗐，我还以为是什么大事，放这儿吧，我来修。
t_en: Those guys can't think outside the box. Of course they can't wrap their heads around my design. *sigh* I thought it was something big. Leave it here. I'll take a look.

t_ch: 麻烦你了。
t_en: Sorry to trouble you.

t_ch: 跟我客气什么？对了，城里突然戒严，是出什么事了吗？
t_en: Why are you being so polite? Oh, and why's the city under curfew all of a sudden? Has something happened?

t_ch: 这…我收到的指令是对外保密……
t_en: I... I've been instructed to keep it quiet.

t_ch: 小杰杰，翅膀硬了啊…看不起我这个庶民姐姐了……
t_en: Lil' Geppie... So grown up now... Looking down on his civilian sister...

t_ch: 你别这样…唉，算了。反正我不说，佩拉也会告诉你的吧……
t_en: Don't say that... *sigh* Forget it. If I don't tell you, Pela will...

t_ch: 昨晚，布洛妮娅小姐突然出现，进入克里珀堡，没跟任何人打招呼。
t_en: Last night, Lady Bronya suddenly appeared. She entered Qlipoth Fort without saying anything to anyone.

t_ch: 大守护者怀疑之前的三个「入侵者」没有落入裂界，而是尾随布洛妮娅返回行政区。她下令全城戒严，逮捕行迹可疑的人。
t_en: The Supreme Guardian is worried that the three intruders may have followed Bronya out of the Fragmentum and back into the Administrative District. She issued a curfew for the entire city, and ordered us to pursue and capture any suspicious individuals.

t_ch: 啊，原来如此，我说布洛妮娅之前怎么没消息呢…她现在怎么样了？
t_en: Ah! So that's how it is, no wonder I haven't heard from Bronya... Is she alright?

t_ch: 不清楚。她没离开克里珀堡，大守护者只说她回来了，没提别的。
t_en: I'm not sure. She hasn't left Qlipoth Fort. The supreme guardian just said she'd returned. Nothing more.

t_ch: 好吧，我还挺担心她的…她不见之后，佩拉说自己的工作量翻了一倍，连出门都没空了。
t_en: Well, I was sure worried about her... After she disappeared, Pela's workload doubled — she said she barely had time to go out.

t_ch: 她还真什么都跟你说啊…啊，对了，上次我把歌德宾馆的年庆限量水壶落在这了，我带走了啊。
t_en: She really does tell you everything... Oh, by the way, I left my Goethe Hotel limited-edition flask behind last time I was here. Let me have a look for it. I think I left it over...

t_ch: 等、等一下！
t_en: W—Wait!

t_ch: 怎么了？姐，你今天真的很不正常……
t_en: What is it? You seem pretty off today, Serval...

t_ch: 那边…哦，对了！你之前不是养死了好几盆花么？我清理屋子的时候把它们都堆到那边了，味道特别大！你不要过去！
t_en: It's, um... right! You remember how you neglected all those flowers to death? I piled them all over there during a bit of spring cleaning, and now they smell terrible! So... don't go over there!

t_ch: …先不说这是什么奇怪的理由——你真的清理过屋子吗？不会又在搞什么被禁止的研究吧……
t_en: ...Seems like a strange reason not to take a look... plus, am I supposed to believe you were spring cleaning? You must be up to more forbidden research...

t_ch: 才没有呢——你这家伙，老姐在你眼里到底是什么形象？
t_en: Am not — how dare you! What do you take your sister for?

t_ch: 唉…算了，我的确不该侵犯你的私人空间。公务在身，我先走了。
t_en: *sigh* Never mind. This is your space, and I shouldn't intrude. I've got business to take care of. Bye for now.

t_ch: 哎！先别走——那个…那些「入侵者」，我就是想问问，他们犯了什么罪？
t_en: Hey! Wait a sec — uh... Those intruders, I wanted to ask. What crime have they committed?

t_ch: 蓄谋颠覆筑城者，阴谋破坏贝洛伯格。
t_en: They're plotting to overthrow the Architects and bring harm to the city.

t_ch: 嘿…和处分我的理由差不多啊。可可利亚的手段一点没变。
t_en: Huh... reminds me of the accusations against me. Cocolia's methods haven't changed.

t_ch: 不要这么说，姐姐。我知道你对大守护者心怀不满，但这开不得玩笑。
t_en: Don't say that, Serval. I know that you're still nursing a grievance against the supreme guardian, but this isn't a joking matter.

t_ch: …又被你教训了。行吧老弟，你慢点走，下次来听我跟佩拉排练。
t_en: ...You're telling me off again. Fine, whatever bro. You better be going, come listen to Pela and my next rehearsal.

t_ch: 有空的话就来。
t_en: I will... if I have the time.

t_ch: 出来吧，现在应该安全了。
t_en: The coast is clear. You can come out now.

t_ch: 呼…憋死我了！这什么电视剧的桥段……
t_en: Phew...I nearly suffocated! What kind of plot device was that...

t_ch: 老弟去拿水壶的时候，我才是真的提心吊胆啊……
t_en: I almost lost my cool when he went for his flask...

t_ch: …布洛妮娅有危险，不行，我们得去救她。
t_en: ...This is bad. Bronya's in danger. We have to go save her.

t_ch: 就我们这几个人去闯克里珀堡？还是打消这个念头吧，希儿姑娘，你本事再大，我也不能放任你去送死啊。
t_en: Attempting to storm Qlipoth Fort by ourselves? Forget it, Seele. I don't care how skilled you are. I'm not sending you on a suicide mission.

t_ch: 放心，可可利亚她…她不会对布洛妮娅怎么样的。就算人心善变，至少这点我有信心。
t_en: Rest assured, Cocolia may have changed over the years... but she'd never hurt Bronya. I'm certain of that.

t_ch: …布洛妮娅也说过类似的话，真不明白为什么，你们反对那个可可利亚，某方面又特别相信她。
t_en: ...You sound like Bronya herself. I don't get it — you guys are against Cocolia, and yet in some ways, you seem to trust her completely.

t_ch: 想到说服杰帕德的办法吗？
t_en: Have you thought of a way to persuade Gepard?

t_ch: 刚才试探过了，结果你看到啦？没有铁证拍在脸上，老弟绝不会质疑可可利亚的命令。你们要是先去找了他，计划铁定完蛋。
t_en: I was gauging his reactions just now, did you see? Unless we have irrefutable evidence, he won't question Cocolia's orders. If you go after him now, our plan's as good as over.

t_ch: 杰帕德会去铁卫禁区吗？
t_en: Is Gepard going to the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone?

t_ch: 刚才他都说啦，要先在城里待上一阵。只要我们动作够快，应该就能跟他错开。
t_en: Didn't you hear him? He said he needs to take care of a few matters in the city first. If we're fast enough, we can stay out of his way.

t_ch: 走，我们去铁卫禁区。只要抢先一步找到「星核」，可可利亚就拿你们没办法了。
t_en: Come on, let's go to the restricted zone. If we can get ahead of the curve and find the Stellaron, Cocolia will be out of options.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅，她真的会平安无事吗？
t_en: Is Bronya really safe?

t_ch: 这么关心她，你们是很要好的朋友吗？
t_en: You care about her, huh. You must have become close friends.

t_ch: 要好…还谈不上吧，只是我答应过她……
t_en: Close is... a bit much. But I made her a promise...

t_ch: 放心吧，她是我见过最坚强的姑娘。
t_en: Relax, she's the strongest girl I've ever seen.

t_ch: 嗯…这我清楚。
t_en: Mm... I can believe that.

t_ch: 你听，希露瓦小姐。如果轻轻摇动这个盒子的话，还能听见咣当咣当的水声——
t_en: Listen, Miss Serval. If you shake the box lightly, you can still hear the clanging sound of water —

t_ch: 这八成是销毁莎草纸的醋呢。就是那种，呃…如果强行打开，就会自动销毁文件的小机关。
t_en: It is probably some kind of vinegar that is used to dissolve papyrus. It's sorta like those kinds of mechanisms where the file would be automatically destroyed if it were forcibly opened.

t_ch: 嗯…那确实不能拆——或者说，拆起来比较麻烦。先让我仔细研究一下……
t_en: Hmm... Looks like it shouldn't just be taken apart, or should I say, it'd be hard to non-destructively take it apart. This can be quite the hassle. Let me take a closer look.

t_ch: 所以，我是说，不要再打拆开它的主意啦！
t_en: What I'm trying to say is that we shouldn't even try to take it apart!

t_ch: 你们遇到麻烦了吗？
t_en: Are you guys in trouble?

t_ch: （先不打扰她们了。）
t_en: (Leave them alone for now.)

t_ch: 哟，{NICKNAME}，你怎么来了？有什么东西要修的话就先放那里，等我研究清楚怎么把茉莉小姐的传家宝铁盒拆开再……
t_en: Yo, {NICKNAME}, to what do I owe the pleasure? If you have something that needs fixing, just leave it there. I'll have a look after I first figure out how to take apart Miss Molly's heirloom tin box...

t_ch: <size=36>不！许！拆！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Do! Not! Take! It! Apart!</size>

t_ch: 啊！我、我只是，有点着急…我不是故意的，希露瓦小姐！无意冒犯！
t_en: Oh! I—I'm just, a little anxious... I didn't mean to, Miss Serval! Sorry!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我只是开个玩笑，别放在心上。
t_en: Hahaha, I'm just joking. Rest assured I won't damage it.

t_ch: 你不用回去上班吗，茉莉？
t_en: Don't you have to go back to work, Molly?

t_ch: 是这样的——上次的事情之后，我请了个长假。我觉得自己需要别的事转移一下注意力……
t_en: The thing is... after what happened last time, I took a long vacation. I feel like I need to switch my mind to something else...

t_ch: 毕竟军队里现在只剩烦心事儿了，我还惹上了不该惹的大人物——我也不知道该不该继续待在技术部…唉。
t_en: I mean, there's nothing but chores left for me in the military right now, and also, I messed with some big-wigs that I shouldn't have. I don't know if I should stay in the Technology Division anymore... ugh.

t_ch: 佩拉已经把那件事摆平啦，不用怕。不过我理解你的心情，你就在我的机械屋好好整理一下头绪，想待多久都可以。
t_en: Don't worry. Pela has sorted it all out. But I do understand your concerns. You can think this over in my workshop, and stay as long as you want.

t_ch: 你今天不开张吗，希露瓦？
t_en: Is the shop not opening today, Serval?

t_ch: 当然开张啦。不过最近都没什么生意呢，我就一直在陪这位茉莉小姐研究她的传家宝。
t_en: Of course it's open. But I haven't had much business recently, so I've been helping Miss Molly here with her family heirloom.

t_ch: 不得不说，就算以现在的眼光来看，这小盒子的工艺也十分高级啊——对于这种有趣的东西，我可不能视而不见呢。
t_en: I have to say, even by today's standards, the craftsmanship of this little box is highly advanced. I can't just ignore an item this interesting.

t_ch: 见鬼，你们都不用上班吗…
t_en: Don't you two have work to do?

t_ch: 哈哈，机械屋可是还在营业呢。只是最近都没生意……
t_en: Haha, the workshop is still open. It's just that there's been no business recently...

t_ch: 在上次那件事之后，我请了个长假。我觉得自己需要别的事转移一下注意力。
t_en: After that incident last time, I took a long vacation. I feel like I need to switch my mind to something else.

t_ch: 毕竟军队里现在只剩烦心事儿了，我还惹上了不该惹的大人物——我也不知道该不该继续待在技术部…唉。
t_en: I mean, there's nothing but chores left for me in the military right now, and also, I messed with some big-wigs that I shouldn't have. I don't know if I should stay in the Technology Division anymore... ugh.

t_ch: 佩拉已经把那件事摆平啦，不用怕。不过我理解你的心情，你就在我的机械屋好好整理一下头绪，想待多久都可以。
t_en: Don't worry. Pela has sorted it all out. But I do understand your concerns. You can think this over in my workshop, and stay as long as you want.

t_ch: 总之，如你所见，我和希露瓦小姐正一起研究我的传家宝呢。就是上次事件结束时我们找回来的那个——
t_en: Anyway, as you can see, Miss Serval and I are trying to figure out the mystery about my family heirloom together. The one we brought back from the last incident—

t_ch: 我记得父亲曾经和我说过，「这是祖辈们为我们留下的『保险』——被逼入绝境的时候就想办法打开这个铁盒吧。」
t_en: I remember my father telling me, "This is the 'insurance' left for us by our ancestors... When there is no other way, find a way to open this iron box."

t_ch: 不过，我们除了发现了一道刻在盒子上的谜题外，就没有任何进展了。我是想直接拆开它，但茉莉一直不同意……
t_en: But we haven't made any progress other than finding a puzzle engraved on the box. I wanted to just take it apart, but Molly wouldn't let me...

t_ch: 唉，真是…走投无路的人哪还有心思玩解谜游戏啊，这根本就是在给祖孙后代添堵嘛！
t_en: Ugh... What were my ancestors thinking? Why would anyone in dire need have the time to solve puzzles? Way to mess with their children!

t_ch: 谜题的谜面是什么？
t_en: So what does the puzzle say?

t_ch: 这就是问题所在——我们现在甚至连谜面都看不懂。对我们来说，这就只是一串没见过的字母罢了。
t_en: That's the problem. We can't even read the puzzle. To us, it's just a bunch of letters that we've never seen before.

t_ch: 祖先没留下打开盒子的钥匙吗？
t_en: Don't they leave a key as well?

t_ch: 这一点我们也想到了——按理来说，这样的盒子制作出来后，就只能用对应的钥匙才能打开。
t_en: We thought about that. Logically speaking, a box like this can only be opened with the corresponding key.

t_ch: 但这盒子用的根本不是普通的弹子锁，而且锁孔形状非常奇怪。与其说是钥匙，不如说像是螺丝、或者钻头一类的东西……
t_en: But this box doesn't use a pin tumbler lock mechanism at all, and the shape of the keyhole is so odd. Seems like what it needs is not so much a key, but something like a screw or a drill...

t_ch: 茉莉小姐的祖先显然没给我们留下这种奇形怪状的钥匙，导致我们现在就只能硬着头皮解谜。但无论如何，都得先把谜面解读出来才行。
t_en: Apparently, Miss Molly's ancestors didn't leave us any such oddly shaped key, so we have to bite the bullet and solve the puzzle. But in any case, we have to decipher what the puzzle is first.

t_ch: 解读、解读…对啊！希露瓦小姐，{NICKNAME}，你们说，这会不会是铁卫的加密技术？毕竟我们家世世代代都在技术部任职，会用到铁卫的密码也很正常吧？
t_en: Decipher... Oh, yes! Miss Serval, {NICKNAME}, could this be the Silvermane Guards encryption technology? After all, our family has worked in the Technology Division for generations, so it won't be a stretch to presume that my ancestors used codes from the Guard, right?

t_ch: 有道理。但我早就被铁卫开除了，这种东西我完全没有印象……
t_en: Makes sense. But I was expelled from the Guard a long time ago. I have no idea about that kind of thing...

t_ch: 啊，我只是听情报部的同事聊到过这个，具体内容我也不清楚。毕竟我只是基层员工，平时工作里也遇不到这些东西。
t_en: Oh, I only once heard my colleagues from the Intelligence Department talking about this. I don't know the specifics. After all, I am just an entry-level employee, and I don't usually encounter these things at work.

t_ch: 这下又没头绪了啊…{NICKNAME}，你还有什么想法吗？
t_en: I'm out of ideas now... {NICKNAME}, what do you think?

t_ch: 铁卫的密码…要不问问杰帕德？
t_en: Silvermane Guards... Should we ask Captain Gepard for help?

t_ch: 哈哈，指望我老弟还不如指望我去偷一份对译表呢。他平时都是把这种活交给佩拉的……
t_en: Haha, you wanna ask my brother for help? It'd be much easier to steal the Silvermane Guards' master cipher codebook. My brother wouldn't know a thing about encryption. He usually just leaves this kind of work to Pela...

t_ch: 情报部的同事…要不问问佩拉？
t_en: Intelligence Department. Maybe ask Pela for help?

t_ch: 希露瓦，拆铁盒就拜托你了…
t_en: So, Serval, please dismantle the tin box...

t_ch: 都说了不许拆……
t_en: No! I told you, we're not gonna take it apart...

t_ch: 哈哈，{M#他}{F#她}应该是开玩笑的啦。这时候要是佩拉在就好了，她脑子转得快……
t_en: Haha, {M#he}{F#she} is only joking. If only Pela were here. She's always clever with these sorta things...

t_ch: 对啊，佩拉！我怎么把她忘了呢——她不就是情报专家嘛！
t_en: You are right, Pela! How could I forget. She's an intelligence expert!

t_ch: 啊，佩拉小姐上午出门前好像说了，她要去买新刊……
t_en: Oh, I believe Miss Pela mentioned that she would get a new magazine just before she left this morning.

t_ch: 新刊？哼哼，那我明白了…她休息日经常一整天都泡在书商那里。走，我们找她去。
t_en: New magazine, huh? I see... She often spends her off days at the bookstore. Come on, let's go find her.

t_ch: 那能用钥匙打开吗？
t_en: Can it really be opened with a key?

t_ch: 要不…咱就直接把它拆开呗？
t_en: Or... we could just dismantle it?

t_ch: 不行不行！这样会把内容物毁掉的……
t_en: No no! That will destroy the contents inside...

t_ch: 嗯…我觉得还是有办法安全拆开它的。你看这里的结构……
t_en: Well... I think there's still a way to non-destructively take it apart. You see the structure here...

t_ch: …希露瓦小姐！
t_en: ...Miss Serval!

t_ch: 嗨，菲丝！最近生意怎么样？
t_en: Hi Fizz! How's business?

t_ch: 呀，这不是希露瓦嘛。生意嘛，还是马马虎虎…不过能像这样填饱肚子之后悠闲地读书，倒也无所谓啦。
t_en: Aha, if it isn't Serval. Business? Same old... But it's a nice way to earn a living. Every now and then, I can read leisurely like this.

t_ch: 啊，你是来取那本书的吧？不好意思呢，出版社那边似乎出了点问题，我还没收到货呢。
t_en: Ah, are you here to pick up that book? Sorry, there seems to be something wrong with the publisher. I haven't received it yet.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我的事不急，慢慢来吧。我来找佩拉，她之前来过了吗？
t_en: Well, no worries. It's not like I need the book right away. I actually came to see Pela. Was she here earlier today?

t_ch: 佩拉小姐？啊～她刚回去取钱啦，应该就快回来了吧。
t_en: Miss Pela? Ah! She just went back to get money. She should be back soon.

t_ch: 什么书这么贵啊？
t_en: Are books sold here that expensive?

t_ch: 她居然也有没带够钱的时候…
t_en: Can't believe that she didn't bring enough money...

t_ch: 是这样的——佩拉小姐不知道这次的新刊还有会场限定附刊，所以只带了买普通版的钱……
t_en: Here is what happened. Miss Pela wasn't aware that this new magazine has a venue-limited edition, and she only brought enough money for the regular edition...

t_ch: 那个…这到底是什么杂志？会场又是什么？
t_en: So... what kind of magazine is this? What is the venue?

t_ch: 我猜又是那什么…《雪国冒险奇谭》粉丝们搞的二次创作吧，小说和连环画都有，就是写点小说人物之间的，呃…爱恨情仇。
t_en: I am guessing it's fan fiction of that... Tale of the Winterlands. They have both novels and comics. They write about the... relationship... between the characters from the original novels.

t_ch: 不过这些内容都是粉丝们幻想出来的，有的甚至和原著基本上没关系了。他们好像定期会举办交流会，用来交流心得感悟。
t_en: But they are all created by fans, and some of them are completely unrelated to the original works. I think they have meetings on a regular basis to share their ideas.

t_ch: 佩拉她可是《雪国冒险奇谭》书迷会里的风云人物呢——他们都管她叫作…唔，什么来着……
t_en: Pela is an important member of the Tale of the Winterlands fan club. They all call her... um, what was it...

t_ch: 没想到佩拉还擅长这种事情…
t_en: I never knew Pela was good at this kind of thing...

t_ch: 怎么说呢…厉害的人，果然不管做什么都很厉害啊。
t_en: Well... Smart people are just good at everything.

t_ch: 铁卫的生活真是丰富多彩啊。
t_en: The Guards really live interesting lives

t_ch: 唉，可能铁卫全军里只有我一个人整天和同事怄气了。
t_en: *sigh* Maybe I'm the only one in the Guards who spends all day sulking.

t_ch: 唉，阿尔乔姆，你好温柔…
t_en: Oh, Artem, you are so gentle...

t_ch: 咦？你说什么？
t_en: Huh? What did you say?

t_ch: 嘿，看来你和佩拉很有共同语言啊，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Hey, looks like you and Pela have a lot in common, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 哟，菲丝，我回来了～
t_en: Yo Fizz, I'm back!

t_ch: 啊，正好，佩拉小姐回来了！
t_en: Ah, right on time, Miss Pela is back!

t_ch: …你们怎么都在这里啊？！
t_en: ... Why are you all here!?

t_ch: 菲丝，实在不好意思，那本限定场刊先帮我留着吧…我之后再回来拿——那个…你刚刚没让他们看到新刊内容吧？
t_en: So, Fizz... Um... This is kinda embarrassing, but could you do me a favor and reserve that limited edition for me... Seems like I'll have to buy it a bit later. Also... You didn't let them see the new edition, did you?

t_ch: 当然没有啦，嘿嘿。
t_en: Of course not, hehe.

t_ch: 那就好。谢谢你，菲丝，我身为情报官的威严保住了……
t_en: Oh, good. Thank you, Fizz. My dignity as an intelligence officer has been saved...

t_ch: 所以你们跑到这里是来做什么的？不要和我说是来买书的，我不信。
t_en: So what are you guys doing here? Don't tell me you're here to buy books 'cuz I won't believe it.

t_ch: 我们想请你帮忙破译密码。
t_en: We need your help to decipher something.

t_ch: 是的，我和希露瓦小姐在我的传家宝铁盒上发现了一串密码。请问佩拉小姐可以帮忙看看吗？
t_en: Yep. You see, Miss Serval and I found a line of code on my heirloom tin box. Can you take a look, Miss Pela?

t_ch: 我想拜读佩拉小姐的大作…
t_en: I wanna read Miss Pela's masterpiece...

t_ch: …希露瓦。
t_en: ...Serval.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，怎么啦？佩拉小姐～
t_en: Yes? How can I help, Miss Pela~

t_ch: 今天这件事，我们回了机械屋再算总账……
t_en: Serval, you big mouth! I'll sort you out once we get back to the workshop...

t_ch: 啊，那个…！其实是我和希露瓦小姐在我的传家宝铁盒上发现了一串密码，请问佩拉小姐可以帮忙看看吗？
t_en: Um... Actually, Miss Serval and I found a line of code on my heirloom tin box. Miss Pela, can you please take a look?

t_ch: 可以是可以…那我们回机械屋详细说吧。
t_en: I mean, I could... Let's go back to the workshop and talk about it in detail.

t_ch: 咦，佩拉小姐不在这里吗？
t_en: Oh, Miss Pela isn't here?

t_ch: 我们去找菲丝问问看吧。
t_en: Let's go and ask Fizz.
*sigh*

t_ch: 你就是技术部的茉莉小姐吧？情况还好吗？
t_en: You must be Molly from the Technology Division. How are things going?

t_ch: 离我远点！烦死了！
t_en: Leave me alone! So annoying!

t_ch: 欸？原来是佩拉长官…对不起，我、我遇到了一些突发状况…万分抱歉！
t_en: Huh? Oh, you're Officer Pela... I'm sorry, I—I'm in the middle of an emergency... I am very sorry!

t_ch: 啊，你也来了…那些残次品机器人，都处理好了吗？
t_en: Ah, you're here as well, I see... Have those defective robots been dealt with?

t_ch: 都办妥了，没人受伤。
t_en: All done. No one was hurt.

t_ch: 那就好……
t_en: That's good...

t_ch: 不对，关我什么事？我根本就是在帮那帮人擦屁股…替他们操心那么多干什么？又不是我的错！
t_en: No, wait. Why should it have anything to do with me? All I ended up doing was just cleaning up the mess left behind by those guys... Why should I worry so much for them? It's not like it's my fault!

t_ch: 你家里有没有人受伤？
t_en: Did anyone in your family get hurt?

t_ch: …算了，那群人的死活和我有什么关系。
t_en: ... Forget it. Why do I care whether they live or die?

t_ch: 家？…哦，原来两位是为这件事来的啊。还好，我一个人住，没有牵连别人受伤。
t_en: Family? Oh, is this what you two are here for? Fortunately, I live alone, and no one else got hurt.

t_ch: 我刚才正在军火仓库等你的消息，结果就被那帮人找上门了。他们说，我这次做得太过火了，所以要给我一个警告…我立马意识到事情不对，就拼命跑回家——
t_en: I was waiting for your update at the arsenal just now, and those people came to me. They said that I went too far this time, so they gave me a warning... I realized something was wrong, so I ran home as fast as I could —

t_ch: 结果，等我赶回家里的时候…整个屋子已经被他们翻了个底朝天了！所有值钱的、不值钱的…全都、全都被他们抢走了啊！
t_en: By the time I got home, the whole house had been turned upside down by them! All the valuables, and even the worthless stuff are gone... Everything. Gone. They took everything!

t_ch: 他们甚至还蹬鼻子上脸，在门窗墙壁上写满了侮辱我的话——
t_en: They even wrote insults all over the doors, windows, and walls —

t_ch: ——可我一点办法都没有！难道就只能放任他们胡作非为吗…就没有人能治治他们吗？
t_en: But I can't do anything about it! Am I supposed to just let them get away with it... Can't anyone hold them accountable for their actions?

t_ch: 到底为什么…我只是想做好自己的本职工作啊！凭什么我就一定得受这样的羞辱？我到底做错了什么？！
t_en: Why... I was just doing my job! Why do I have to be humiliated? What did I do wrong!?

t_ch: （任由她哭喊发泄）
t_en: (Let her cry and vent.)

t_ch: 可恶！这世道已经没有正义可言了吗？呜……
t_en: Darn it! Is there no justice in this world? *sobbing*...

t_ch: 我们去帮你拿回属于你的东西。
t_en: We'll help you take back what is rightfully yours.

t_ch: 这事因我而起，你没必要做到这份上…我只是需要发泄一下……
t_en: This happened all because of me. You don't have to do that... I just need to blow off some steam...

t_ch: 别怕，茉莉小姐…这件事并不是你的错，交给我们处理就好——铁卫上层现在已经介入这起事故了。更何况你刚遭到报复，还是别和那帮恶棍硬碰硬为好。
t_en: Don't be afraid, Miss Molly... This isn't your fault. Just let us handle this from now on. We the Silvermane Guards are now officially on this case. For now, it's best for you not to directly confront those thugs since they've just shown they are not shy about using violence.

t_ch: 另外，铁卫绝对不会坐视你无家可归的。你可以…呃…先到「永动」机械屋寻求暂时庇护。
t_en: Oh, another thing, you can rest assured that we the Silvermane Guards will never let you go homeless. You can... um... seek temporary shelter in the Neverwinter workshop for now.

t_ch: 机械屋的主人，她有能力确保你的安全。见面之后报我的名字就好。
t_en: The owner of the workshop can ensure your safety. Just say my name when you meet her.

t_ch: …可我想和你们一起去！我想亲眼看到那帮恶棍被绳之以法……
t_en: ... But I want to go with you! I want to see those gang of thugs brought to justice with my own eyes...

t_ch: …这太危险了，我们要优先考虑你的人身安全。晚些时候，我会协调人员对你的住处进行修缮，现在还请你专注于保护自己，免遭进一步迫害。
t_en: ...It's just too risky. Your security must be our top priority. I'll arrange to have your house fixed later. For the time being, please just focus on protecting yourself in case of further reprisals.
*sobbing*

t_ch: （她好像还是一副气不过的样子…）
t_en: (She still seems very upset...)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，这次事件的犯人我已经心里有数了…他们的名字都在我的记录册上，一个也跑不了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I already know the people responsible for this... their names are all in my records. They won't get away.

t_ch: 我们走吧，回铁卫禁区一趟，好好会会这群腐败到骨子里的家伙。
t_en: Now, let's go back to the Restricted Zone and give those filthy thugs a proper "greeting."

t_ch: 佩拉，你看过之后有什么头绪了吗？
t_en: So, Pela, now that you had a look, what do you think?

t_ch: 嗯…你们的思路应该是对的——这种不成文的乱序字母确实很像加密后的结果。不过，既然这是六七百年前的东西，那大概用不到我们现在通行的算法。
t_en: Well, I think you guys are probably on the right track. These disorganized letters certainly resemble cryptography. However, given that this was created <unbreak>600</unbreak> or <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago, it most likely uses a completely different encryption algorithm.

t_ch: 啊…那六七百年前的加密算法是什么样的呢？
t_en: So, what encryption algorithm did they use then?

t_ch: 我想想…我记得《银鬃铁卫密码学》第七版第二章第四节是这么写的：「建军初期，情报官兼技术部总负责人理查德•巴拉金在全军推广了最早的加密文法——」
t_en: Let me think... I remember that in Chapter 2, Section 4 of the 7th Edition of the Silvermane Guards Cryptology, it says, "In the early days of the Guards, Richard Balakin, the intelligence officer and chief of the Technology Division, popularized the earliest encryption method within the force."

t_ch: 「明文的所有字母都在字母表上向后偏移四位，依此法即可得到密文；反之亦可用于解密。」
t_en: "All the letters are shifted four places backward in the alphabet, and the ciphertext can be obtained this way; the reverse can also be used for decryption."

t_ch: 那我们来试试看吧。
t_en: So let's try it out.

t_ch: 你怎么记得这么清楚…
t_en: How do you remember that so clearly...

t_ch: 这可是身为情报官的基本素养，可不要小瞧我哦。
t_en: That's just one of the basic skills of an intelligence officer. So don't underestimate me.

t_ch: 那么，按照这个方法的话…有了——「关键」…「在群山的隘口」…「等候」，「彼处为」…「大守护者」…「运筹之地」？另外，落款是「理查德•巴拉金」。
t_en: So, according to this method...it should be... I got it! Here it is: "The key"... "At the mountain pass"... "Waiting," "That place is"... "The Supreme Guardian"... "The place of operation"? In addition, the inscription is "Richard Balakin."

t_ch: 看姓氏，这位理查德确实是我的祖辈——等等，这不就是刚刚佩拉小姐提到的那个人吗？而且他到底在说什么谜语？
t_en: Based on the surname, this Richard is indeed my ancestor. Wait, isn't this the guy Miss Pela just mentioned? And what on earth does this mean anyway?

t_ch: 难道是下一条线索的位置？
t_en: Could it be the location of the next clue?

t_ch: 你说得对！这句话中提到了两个地点……
t_en: You are right! There are two locations mentioned in this sentence...

t_ch: 谜语人滚出贝洛伯格！
t_en: Get out of Belobog, Riddler!

t_ch: …「谜语人」是什么？
t_en: ...What is a Riddler?

t_ch: 「群山的隘口」、「大守护者运筹之地」，我猜这指的是那座被裂界侵蚀的边堡要塞？
t_en: "The mountain pass," "the place of operation of The Supreme Guardian," I'm guessing it's referring to the fortress that was eroded by the Fragmentum?

t_ch: 而「关键」指的应该就是打开盒子的钥匙。也就是说，这位祖辈把传家宝铁盒的钥匙藏在了残响回廊里。
t_en: And the "key" must be referring to the key that opens the box. In other words, this ancestor hid the key of the heirloom tin box in the Corridor of Fading Echoes.

t_ch: 完了…别说普通市民了，就算是铁卫也很难踏足那片裂界，更别说到里面去找东西了。
t_en: Uh oh... Even for the Guards, it is difficult to set foot in the Fragmentum, let alone ordinary citizens like us, especially if you want to go searching around inside.

t_ch: 怎么办？好不容易找到了线索，却只能在这一步放弃了吗……
t_en: What do we do? We've finally found a clue. Do we have to give up after all?

t_ch: 我们可以去帮你找。
t_en: We can help you find it.

t_ch: 虽然听说你经常在裂界里往来，但那个地方肯定危机四伏吧……
t_en: Even for a seasoned traveler like you, the Fragmentum must be too perilous to go into, or so I've heard.

t_ch: 裂界就像我家一样…
t_en: I visit the Fragmentum like it's my home...

t_ch: 那你家可能有点不太宜居……
t_en: Then your home must be quite the hell hole...

t_ch: 你真的打算去帮我找「钥匙」吗？太感谢了…不过记得不要勉强，生命安全第一！
t_en: Are you really going to help me find the key? Thank you so much! But I don't wanna pressure you into this. Safety should always be the number one priority!

t_ch: 没关系，我会好好看着这家伙的。茉莉小姐，你和希露瓦一起在机械屋等着就好。
t_en: Don't worry. I'll look after {M#him}{F#her}. Miss Molly, just wait in the workshop with Serval.

t_ch: 事不宜迟，我们现在就出发吧。你准备好了吗，{NICKNAME} ？
t_en: Without further ado, let's go now. Are you ready, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 让我们出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 再让我准备准备…
t_en: Let me get prepared first...

t_ch: 那你准备好了就和我说一声。我就在这里等你。
t_en: Then let me know when you're ready. I'll wait for you right here.

t_ch: 怎么样，你准备好了吗，{NICKNAME} ？
t_en: What's up, are you ready, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 没事，等你准备好再说，我就在这里等你。
t_en: It's okay. I'll wait for you here when you're ready.

t_ch: 看，{F#她们}{M#他们}俩这不是回来了嘛。
t_en: Look, they are back.

t_ch: 哎？你回来了！怎么样，没遇到危险吧？
t_en: Hey? You're back! How was it? Did you run into any danger?

t_ch: 还好，没遇到危险。
t_en: Fortunately, none at all.

t_ch: 那就好！要是你们因为我的事情出了什么意外……
t_en: That's good! If something happens to you because of me...

t_ch: 不说晦气话了，快告诉我发生了什么吧。
t_en: Anyway, enough ifs. Please tell me what happened.

t_ch: 差点死在里边…
t_en: We almost died...

t_ch: 欸，真的假的？要是你们因为我的事出了什么意外，我肯定会……
t_en: What, for real? If anything happens to you because of me, I'll definitely...

t_ch: 算了，不说晦气话了！快告诉我发生了什么吧。
t_en: Never mind, enough ifs. Please tell me what happened.

t_ch: 从老巴拉金的谜题，到有惊无险的栈桥事故，再到奇妙的钻头饰品，你和佩拉一五一十地向二人汇报了此次裂界之旅的见闻。
t_en: From old Balakin's puzzles, to the gear bridge incident, to the strange drill ornaments, you and Pela tell them everything you experienced on this trip to Fragmentum.

t_ch: 还真是有趣的冒险经历呢，可惜我没跟着你们去。干脆下次找个机会去调查一下那些机关，把它们都拆开看看好了～
t_en: What an interesting adventure. Such a pity I didn't go with you. We should find a chance to investigate those mechanisms and dismantle them next time.

t_ch: 那你首先得过杰帕德这关……
t_en: Well, you have to get Gepard's approval first...

t_ch: 总之，两位能平安归来就好，真的非常感谢各位。另外，我也很在意你在那边捡到的东西…快让我看看吧！
t_en: Anyway, I'm glad to see you two back safely. I very much appreciate your help. Plus, I'm also very interested in what you've found over there... Let me take a look!

t_ch: 我捡到一副…肖像画。
t_en: I found a... portrait.

t_ch: 啊！这位巴拉金先辈，年轻的时候还真是…古怪呢。
t_en: Ah! This Balakin was really... eccentric when he was young.

t_ch: 嗯？他制服上的这枚勋章，我好像有印象——这应该是「『造物引擎』计划功勋奖章」。
t_en: Hmm? I seem to remember that medal on his uniform — it must be the medal of honor from the Engine of Creation Project.

t_ch: 而且，根据勋章的造型来看，这位理查德•巴拉金十有八九是『造物引擎』计划的首席工程师之一。
t_en: Also, according to the shape of the medal, this Richard Balakin was probably one of the chief engineers of the Engine of Creation project.

t_ch: 「造物引擎」？那是什么？我好像只在技术部的档案里瞟到过一眼……
t_en: Engine of Creation? What is that? I think I may have once seen it in the Technology Division's archives...

t_ch: 据我所知…目前铁卫能接触到的官方记录里，相关记载也寥寥无几。我只知道这是阿丽萨•兰德时期打造的巨型地质改造单元，而且已经全部遗失了。
t_en: To the best of my knowledge, there are very few relevant records, even in the official documents the Guards have access to. All I know is that this is a giant geological transformation unit built during the Alisa Rand era, and it's all lost now.

t_ch: 原来如此！感觉这位前辈真是不得了，而且还怪喜欢折腾人的…天才的脑回路都是这么神奇吗？
t_en: So I see. This guy was really incredible, and it's also strange that he liked messing with people... Are all geniuses so eccentric?

t_ch: 不管怎么样——谢谢你，{NICKNAME}！这张有趣的照片，我一定会好好珍藏的。
t_en: Anyway, thank you, {NICKNAME}! I will definitely treasure this interesting photo.

t_ch: 我还找到了你的「钥匙」。
t_en: I also found your key.

t_ch: 啊，就是这个！让我看看，这个钻头样的小玩意应该可以完美嵌入铁盒的钥匙孔……
t_en: Oh, this is it! Let me see, this drill-like gadget should fit perfectly into the keyhole of this tin box...

t_ch: 我的手都开始抖了，{NICKNAME}——但愿没有让你白跑一趟……
t_en: My hands are trembling now, {NICKNAME}. I hope your effort won't be wasted...

t_ch: 成、成功了！
t_en: W—we did it!

t_ch: …这是什么？纸条吗？
t_en: ... What's this? A piece of paper?

t_ch: 「改变世界的力量」……
t_en: "The Power to Change the World"...

t_ch: 我…好像明白了。我从小就脾气差，其他孩子都不愿意跟我玩在一块。工作了以后，我也经常因为一些看不过眼的小事跟别人发生争执。
t_en: I... I think I get it now. I've always had a bad temper, ever since I was little, and all the other kids shunned me. Things didn't get better even after I got a job. I've gotten into quite a few fights with people over some very trivial things.

t_ch: 其实我根本不用试图改变别人…这样只会分散我的注意力，凭空消耗我的热情。
t_en: The truth is, I shouldn't even try to change people at all... It's all just a distraction that keeps draining my passion for life itself.

t_ch: 或许…就像祖辈说的：我需要的只是一把扳钳，一枚钻头。
t_en: It's just like what my ancestors said — all I ever need is a wrench and a drill.

t_ch: …我想明白了。我不能再这样下去了。我要辞掉在技术部的工作，找个地方重新开始——这次自立门户。
t_en: ...I have figured it out. I can't go on like this anymore. I'm quitting my job in the Technology Division to start my own thing. This time, I'll make it on my own.

t_ch: 如果我不在乎外界的干扰，只专注于手上的工作，我就能握住自己的未来。或许…或许有一天还能改变这座城市。
t_en: I'll be able to decide my own fate if I ignore the noise from the outside world and just focus on the work I have at hand. One day... maybe... just maybe... I'll be able to bring change to this city.

t_ch: 我支持你！
t_en: I support you!

t_ch: 我的目标就是成为希露瓦小姐这样的人——天不怕地不怕，一心只扑在自己热爱的事业上，还能由此为他人创造价值……
t_en: My goal in life is to be like Miss Serval, who has no fear and pursues her work with such passion, while simultaneously creating value for others.

t_ch: 原来茉莉是这样看待希露瓦的啊…没想到她还有被当成正面典型的一天呢。
t_en: So this is how Molly sees Serval... Never thought she could be a role model for somebody.

t_ch: 当然啦。虽然在本人面前有点难以启齿，但希露瓦小姐其实一直是我的…偶像。可惜不管是天才般的头脑，还是显赫的家世，这些我一样都没有。
t_en: Well, it'd be a bit embarrassing to say it out loud in front of her. Even though I don't have the brain of a genius, nor was I born into an influential family, Miss Serval has always been my role model.

t_ch: 彻底独立可是很难的。
t_en: Total independence is hard to achieve.

t_ch: …你说得对。假如失去了公务身份的保护，我说不定会遭到更可怕的报复。
t_en: ...You are right. If I were to lose my privilege as a public worker, I might face even more horrific retaliation.

t_ch: 啊，要是我能像希露瓦小姐一样就好啦。有朗道家族作后盾的话，也不至于这样束手束脚了……
t_en: Oh, if only I could be like Miss Serval, supported by a family like the Landaus. Then I wouldn't be in so much trouble...

t_ch: 不、不过我一定会努力的！反正我一个人都是这么过来的……
t_en: But... but I will try my best! After all, I've already gone through so much on my own.

t_ch: 说什么呢，茉莉？这话我可不爱听了啊。
t_en: What are you talking about, Molly? I don't like the sound of that.

t_ch: …欸？我说错话了吗？对不起！
t_en: ... Eh? Did I say something wrong? Sorry!

t_ch: 嗯…倒也不能算说错话，只是我必须指出你这番话中的两个问题——
t_en: Well, it's not wrong per se, but I have to point out two inaccuracies in what you said —

t_ch: 第一，我虽然名义上是朗道家的大小姐，但实际上早就被赶出家门了。什么显赫的家室呀，早就是过去式了。
t_en: Firstly, even though I am the eldest daughter of the Landau family, I was actually kicked out of the family a long time ago. All that stuff about prestigious family... it's long been a distant memory.

t_ch: 第二，茉莉——你和我其实没有什么区别。你看，咱俩的经历不是很类似吗？都是在军队里混不下去的天才技术人员，都被逼着逃出来自立门户……
t_en: Secondly, you and I are really not that different. You see, we actually have similar life experiences. We are both genius technicians who can't fit in with the military, and we are both forced to get out and work on our own...

t_ch: 「天才」？不不，我跟希露瓦小姐不一样，根本算不上什么「天才」……
t_en: Genius? No, no, I'm not like you, Serval. I'm no genius...

t_ch: 你怎么还谦虚上了？听{NICKNAME}说，你以前可不是这样哦。
t_en: Why are you being so modest now? I heard from {NICKNAME} that you weren't like this before.

t_ch: 啊…希露瓦小姐——
t_en: Uh... Miss Serval...

t_ch: 请你…请你收我做助手吧！
t_en: Please... please take me in as your assistant!

t_ch: 这才对嘛，这才是年轻有为的机械师该有的精气神！我就等你这句话呢！
t_en: That's right, that's the spirit of a young and promising mechanic! I've been waiting for you to say this!

t_ch: 为了庆祝希露瓦机械屋迎来第一位助手——就由我来给各位演奏一曲吧！来，佩拉，把你的架子鼓搬出来——
t_en: To celebrate the first assistant of Serval's Workshop, let me play a song for everyone! Come on, Pela, get your drum set up here.

t_ch: 好吧好吧…真拿你没办法。不过先让我去菲丝那里一趟，我心心念念的限定场刊还没拿到手呢……
t_en: Alright alright... Seems like I can't get out of this one. But let me go to Fizz's place first. I have yet to get my hands on that limited edition magazine yet...

t_ch: 好耶，我要坐前排！
t_en: Okay. I want a front-row seat!

t_ch: 其实我对佩拉的场刊很感兴趣…
t_en: I'm actually very interested in Pela's magazine.

t_ch: …别以为我不知道你在打什么算盘！你们——尤其是你，{NICKNAME}——不许跟过来！绝对不许！
t_en: ... Don't think I don't know what you're up to! You guys — especially you, {NICKNAME} — you are not allowed to follow me! Absolutely not!

t_ch: 怎么还没回来？不会出事了吧……
t_en: Why aren't they back yet? Nothing bad happened to them, right?

t_ch: 放心吧，有佩拉在呢，{F#她们}{M#他们}俩肯定没问题。
t_en: Don't worry. I trust Pela is capable of handling anything. They will be fine.

t_ch: 啊，是杰帕德！他来迎接我们啦？
t_en: Ah, it's Gepard! Is he here to meet us?

t_ch: 还能执行公务，看来他的伤势不算太重。
t_en: He's still on duty. Huh, he must have recovered quickly.

t_ch: 走吧，过去跟他打个招呼。
t_en: Let's go say hi.

t_ch: 「开拓者」，欢迎回到行政区。
t_en: Welcome back to the Administrative District, Trailblazers.

t_ch: 「开拓者」…在这星球上还是第一次被这么称呼欸！
t_en: Trailblazers... first time anyone's called us that on this planet!

t_ch: 筑城者们认为，有必要以更符合礼节的方式称呼各位。「外来者」这几个字，听上去终究有些疏远。
t_en: The Architects think it's only right that we should address you properly. "Outsiders" is hardly fitting anymore.

t_ch: …此前在禁区发生的事，请允许我再次向各位致以歉意。你们并非用心险恶之人…我理应更加相信希露瓦的判断才对。
t_en: ...Please allow me to apologize again for what happened in the restricted zone. You were only trying to help... I should've trusted Serval's judgment.

t_ch: 你只是在履行应尽的职责。
t_en: You were just doing your job.

t_ch: 感谢你们的理解。
t_en: Thanks for understanding.

t_ch: 不听姐姐言…
t_en: Your sister knows what's up...

t_ch: 谢谢你的提醒，我会好好考虑的。
t_en: I'll keep that in mind, thanks.

t_ch: 哈哈，被我们打服了吧？
t_en: Ha! Didn't expect to get wrecked, huh?

t_ch: 你们的实力确实令人钦佩。若非知道你们有要事在身，我很希望你们能协助铁卫训练。
t_en: You are excellent fighters. I'd love to have you provide training for the Guards, but I know you have places to be.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅大人的上任宣讲很快就要开始了。我正是受她之托，特地前来告知各位的。
t_en: Lady Bronya's inaugural address will begin soon. She asked me to pass on her invitation to you.

t_ch: 咦…这么快就准备好了嘛？她的身体状况……
t_en: Huh? That's sooner than I expected. Is she well enough to...

t_ch: 不是很好…但布洛妮娅大人坚持要尽快完成宣讲，给城中的人民一个交代。
t_en: Not really... But Lady Bronya insisted on delivering the speech as soon as possible. She wants the people to understand what happened.

t_ch: 宣讲将在永冬铭碑前举行。届时，希望三位都能到场见证。
t_en: The address will be held at Everwinter Monument. I hope the three of you can find time to attend.

t_ch: 看！那边聚集了好多市民呀，宣讲快开始了吗？
t_en: Look! There's a crowd gathering over there. Is the address starting soon?

t_ch: 嗯，但布洛妮娅似乎还没到场。先找个地方歇息片刻吧。
t_en: Looks like it. Bronya hasn't arrived yet, though. Let's find somewhere to wait.

t_ch: 铁卫，列队！
t_en: Guards, formation!

t_ch: 啊，是要开始了吗？
t_en: Whoa, this must be it.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅•兰德大人的上任宣讲即将开始——请各位市民保持安静，协助维持现场秩序！
t_en: Citizens of Belobog, I kindly ask for your attention! Lady Bronya Rand's inaugural address will begin shortly. Please remain orderly!

t_ch: 贝洛伯格的市民们，请听我说——
t_en: People of Belobog — hear me!

t_ch: 我们今天之所以能聚在这里，共同庆祝这个时刻…是因为一位伟大的「守护者」牺牲了自己，将七百年来一直荼毒这座城市的祸种逐出了我们的家园。
t_en: Today we gather here to celebrate our victory... but it was a sacrifice made by a mighty guardian that delivered us this opportunity. She dispelled an evil that cost us <unbreak>700</unbreak> years of suffering... and in the process, she paid the ultimate price.

t_ch: 可可利亚•兰德的牺牲为我们翻开了历史的新篇章。城墙外的风雪将会逐渐平息，裂界的扩张也必将被铁卫的攻势抑制。
t_en: The sacrifice of Cocolia Rand ushers in a new chapter. The blizzard beyond the walls will die away and the offensives of our Silvermane Guards will begin to contain the Fragmentum's advance.

t_ch: 在极北之地，我亲眼见证了前任大守护者与不可名状的力量展开的抗争。在生命的最后，她也向我倾诉了自己最大的遗憾……
t_en: In the far north, I witnessed with my own eyes the struggle between the late supreme guardian and the evil power that sought to destroy us. In her last moments, the supreme guardian told me her greatest regret...

t_ch: …那就是隔绝了上下层区十余年的封锁令。随着时间的推移，她开始认识到自己的决策带来的严重后果…她虽心存愧疚，却早已无法回头。
t_en: ...The order to seal off the Underworld from the Overworld. With the passage of time, she gradually came to realize the consequences of that decision... Although tortured with guilt, she could not undo the damage.

t_ch: 因此，她在临终前留下嘱托…在「星核」的影响消散之际，她希望贝洛伯格能够重归完整。她希望上下层区的人民能共同迎接黎明。
t_en: In her final moments, she wanted us to mark the Stellaron's end by reuniting Belobog. Overworlders and Underworlders must embrace the dawn of a new age... together.

t_ch: 下层区的各位——我无法奢求你们的原谅，因为亲身的经历已经告诉我，这十余载的岁月给你们留下了太多伤疤。
t_en: People of the Underworld, I dare not ask for your forgiveness — I now know the reality of your decade of suffering...

t_ch: …但我渴求你们的合作。因为我们都十分清楚…缺少了你们的坚毅和汗水，贝洛伯格的复兴将无从谈起…文明的存续也将无从谈起。
t_en: But I want us to work together. We know that without your perseverance and hard work, Belobog will not recover its former glory... and the seed of civilization will not be preserved.

t_ch: 另外…我还想借此机会，向贝洛伯格的几位客人表示感谢。
t_en: ...And on that note, I'd like to extend my heartfelt gratitude to a few guests of the city.

t_ch: 你们中的大多数并不知晓他们的存在…但正是他们的到来，为这个濒临枯萎的世界浇灌了希望。
t_en: Most of you were not aware of their arrival... but it is thanks to them that our world now flourishes with hope.

t_ch: 也正是他们的到来让我意识到，在着眼于「存护」脚下这片土地的同时，我们也不该忘记仰望天空。
t_en: They made me realize that while we remain focused on "preserving" the ground beneath our feet, we should not forget to look up into the sky.

t_ch: 我坚定不移地相信，在你我共同的努力下，我们的世界——被「开拓者」们称之为「雅利洛-Ⅵ」的这个世界——必将重新投身星辰的怀抱。
t_en: I firmly believe that with the determination of everyone, our world, the world the Trailblazers call "Jarilo-VI," will no longer be alone among the stars.

t_ch: 但在完成这个目标之前，请允许我暂时以谦卑的身份引领大家前进……
t_en: But before we accomplish that goal, I humbly ask you to follow me into the future...

t_ch: 我，布洛妮娅•兰德，贝洛伯格第十九任「守护者」，在此宣誓——
t_en: I, Bronya Rand, the nineteenth guardian of Belobog, hereby take my oath...

t_ch: 布洛妮娅大人！布洛妮娅大人！贝洛伯格，永屹不倒！
t_en: Lady Bronya! Lady Bronya! Long live Belobog!

t_ch: 又回到这间办公室了…想起上一次来到这里，仿佛就是上次。
t_en: Back in this office again... The last time I was here was... the last time I was here.

t_ch: 这回感觉不到奇怪的视线啦！
t_en: No more unnerving gaze this time! Hehe...

t_ch: 布洛妮娅！我们回来啦。
t_en: Bronya, we're back!

t_ch: 三月，丹恒，还有{NICKNAME}——你们能赶上宣讲真是太好了。
t_en: March, Dan Heng, {NICKNAME}... it's wonderful you made it to the address.

t_ch: 抱歉，我知道日程安排得过于紧密…但我不想让市民们等太久。无端的猜疑越多，局面就越容易失去控制。
t_en: Sorry, I know the invitation was very last minute... but I needed to get the news out as soon as possible. The longer people waited, the more rumors started to swirl. The situation risked spinning out of control.

t_ch: 身体完全恢复了吗？
t_en: Are you fully recovered?

t_ch: 嗯，已经没事了，请放心。
t_en: Yes, I'm fine now. Don't worry.

t_ch: 那个时候…母亲尝试着将「星核」的声音也植入我的脑海。那种感受…是撕心裂肺般的折磨。幸好你们及时赶到。
t_en: My mother... she tried to force the voice of the Stellaron into my head... It was a terrible feeling. I don't know what would've happened if you hadn't shown up.

t_ch: 宣讲的时候好像没看到希儿。
t_en: Was Seele not there for the address?

t_ch: 她在现场，只是待在你们注意不到的地方。
t_en: She was there. She just made sure no one could see her.

t_ch: 希儿才刚刚离开那种闭塞的环境，不习惯这种场面也很正常…我会带她慢慢适应的。
t_en: Seele is still new to the vastness of the Overworld — she feels uncomfortable in a big crowd... I'll help her adjust to life here.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅的讲话还真是振奋人心呀！换成我的话，肯定紧张得站都站不稳啦。
t_en: That was some speech, Bronya! If I had to talk like that in front of a huge crowd, I'd be shaking head to toe!

t_ch: 不过是在用表面美好的话术编织谎言而已。希望你永远不用经历这些，三月。
t_en: It was all just a bunch of palatable lies. I hope you never have a similar experience, March.

t_ch: 重振文明的决心是真的。
t_en: The part about preserving the seed of civilization was real.

t_ch: 领袖论迹不论心。
t_en: It's what you do as a leader that matters.

t_ch: 善意的谎言也是必须的。
t_en: Sometimes people need to hear white lies.

t_ch: 嗯。我想永远对民众保持诚实…但我更想「存护」住他们的希望。
t_en: Mm... I'd prefer to be honest with the people... but I also want to preserve their hope.

t_ch: …丹恒，那个…杨叔的结论现在要说吗？
t_en: ...Dan Heng, wanna... talk about Mr. Yang's findings?

t_ch: 嗯，由我来转述吧。
t_en: Mm, allow me to explain.

t_ch: 将瓦尔特的观测结果转述给了布洛妮娅……
t_en: Telling Bronya about Welt's observations...

t_ch: 也就是说，裂界的影响已经扎根过深，就算现在封印了星核，也没法即刻逆转……
t_en: ...Which means the Fragmentum has already taken root by this point. Even though the Stellaron is sealed, we won't be able to reverse the damage immediately...

t_ch: 星核就是这么可怕的东西。
t_en: Stellarons are terrible things.

t_ch: 我明白。前人如果不是面对毁灭的威胁，也不会冒险使用它。
t_en: I know. Our ancestors wouldn't have risked using one if they'd had other ways of avoiding calamity.

t_ch: 抱歉，我们之前承诺过…
t_en: Sorry, we promised you...

t_ch: 不，你们不必道歉。
t_en: No... No need for apologies.

t_ch: 为了让这个世界免受劫难，各位已经倾注了所有努力，{NICKNAME}甚至险些为了它牺牲性命——我无权要求你们付出更多。
t_en: You've gone out of your way to save this world. {NICKNAME}, you nearly lost your life. I have no right to ask for more.

t_ch: 而且，那位瓦尔特先生的话我很赞同呀。贝洛伯格的命运，最终还是得由她的子民掌握……
t_en: Besides, I agree with Mr. Yang. Ultimately, the fate of Belobog is in the hands of its own people...

t_ch: 我们会找到根除裂界的方法。
t_en: We will find a way to root out the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 列车组会尽可能帮助你们。
t_en: The Astral Express will do everything within our power to help.

t_ch: 那…作为「领袖」，我也必须给出与之对等的承诺才行。
t_en: In that case... as a leader, I must give you a promise of the same weight.

t_ch: 我们一定会尽全部的努力，坚持到「开拓者」返航的那天。就算要再花上七百个年头…雅利洛-Ⅵ的孩子也会一直仰望星空，心怀希望。
t_en: We will hold out with everything we have until the day you Trailblazers return. Even if it takes another <unbreak>700</unbreak> years, the children of Jarilo-VI will look up to the sky with hope in their hearts.

t_ch: 再见了，朋友们。愿星空「存护」你们的旅途。
t_en: So long, my friends. May the Preservation bless your journey.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，恭喜呀！你的第一次「开拓之旅」，到此就顺利结束啦！
t_en: Congrats, {NICKNAME}! That was one heck of a first trailblazing expedition!

t_ch: 确实值得庆贺。
t_en: Worth celebrating, I'd say.

t_ch: 从一到五，给这次旅程打分…
t_en: On a scale from 1 to 5, how would you rate this journey...

t_ch: 二点五。五！
t_en: Two point five. Five!

t_ch: …什么啊，居然连及格分都没有吗？
t_en: ...What!? That run-of-the-mill, huh?

t_ch: 我的评判标准和你不同。
t_en: My scoring criteria are different from yours.

t_ch: 嘁！还是采访一下{NICKNAME}吧——有什么获奖感言吗？
t_en: Ugh! Anyway, we should be asking {NICKNAME}... Any thoughts you want to share?

t_ch: 让我睡觉……
t_en: Time for a nap...

t_ch: 什么什么？那可不行，眼看咱都要离开了，我连像样的照片都没拍过几张呢。
t_en: Wait, what? No way — we're about to leave and I've hardly got any decent photos.

t_ch: 走吧，我们再去行政区溜达一圈！
t_en: Come on, let's go back to the Administrative District and hang out for a bit longer!

t_ch: 还能再战八百回合！
t_en: I feel like I could fight a hundred more monsters!

t_ch: 真精神呀，要的就是这股劲！走吧——回列车之前，我还得多拍几张纪念照才行。
t_en: That's the spirit! Come on, we should take some more photos before we head back to the Express.

t_ch: 出发，再去行政区溜达一圈！
t_en: Let's go! One more round in the Administrative District!

t_ch: 下一次会是怎样的呢？
t_en: What will the next adventure be like?

t_ch: 肯定也会很难忘吧，哎呀，现在别想那么远，珍惜当下！
t_en: Equally unforgettable, I'm sure. Anyway, don't worry about the future — live in the moment!

t_ch: 走吧，我们再去行政区溜达一圈！我还想多拍几张纪念照呢。
t_en: Let's go for one more round in the Administrative District! I wanna take more photos!

t_ch: 宣讲快开始了吗？
t_en: Is the address starting soon?

t_ch: 可可利亚大人居然…
t_en: Madam Cocolia is...

t_ch: 真是历史性的时刻啊。
t_en: What a historic moment.

t_ch: 咦？你是下层区人？
t_en: Huh? You're from the Underworld?

t_ch: …好多铁卫，好帅气！
t_en: ...So many handsome Silvermane Guards!

t_ch: 没想到啊，我居然…
t_en: I never thought that I would...

t_ch: 居然能活到这个时候…
t_en: ...be able to survive up till now.

t_ch: 缆车站重新开放了？
t_en: Is the cable car station operating?

t_ch: 已经这么久了啊…
t_en: It's been so long...

t_ch: 布洛妮娅说，愿意为了我们再等上七百年……
t_en: Bronya said she's willing to wait <unbreak>700</unbreak> years for us...

t_ch: 唔…丹恒，最长寿的人类能活到多少岁呀？
t_en: Hmm... Dan Heng, how long can humans live for?

t_ch: …无穷无尽。但，那已经不能称之为人类了。
t_en: ...Forever. But in that scenario, they can't be considered human anymore.

t_ch: 我就问问，你别突然这么严肃啊……
t_en: Yikes! So serious! I was just curious.

t_ch: 呵…照片拍完了，街也逛累了，接下来……
t_en: *yawn* ... That's enough sightseeing and photo-taking to last me a while...

t_ch: …接下来，就该去歌德宾馆好好睡上一觉了！
t_en: ...Time to head back to the Goethe Hotel for a good night's sleep!

t_ch: …回列车再睡。
t_en: ...We can sleep when we're back on the Express.

t_ch: 什么啊，之前都住进豪华客房了，却被那个坏女人给算计了一把…你们不觉得不甘心吗？
t_en: Not so fast! Last time we were here, that evil Madam Guardian interrupted us before we'd gotten the most out of our luxury suites... Don't you think we should make up for it?

t_ch: 走啦走啦，才一晚而已——姬子和杨叔都是成熟的大人，很有耐心的！
t_en: Come onnnn! Himeko and Mr. Yang are grown-ups — I'm sure they'll cope without us for one night.

t_ch: 上床休息。
t_en: Go to bed.

t_ch: 再随便逛逛。
t_en: Walk around a bit more.

t_ch: 造物引擎、可可利亚、虚幻的宇宙空间、「存护」的视线——与那场决战相关的画面不断闪回交错，最终拼凑成了一段模糊且不真实的记忆胶片。
t_en: The Engine of Creation, Cocolia, an ethereal outer space, the gaze of Preservation... your fragmented memory of the battle forms a blurry and surreal montage.

t_ch: 画面的最后一帧定格在了星核随可可利亚消散前的一秒。那短暂的一瞬，似乎有某个陌生的声音贴近了你的耳边…然后低语……
t_en: In the midst of this frantic tapestry of events, you see the moment Cocolia perished with the Stellaron. A strange voice speaks to you in a hushed tone...
